channel.title: NPR Topics: Health
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: New research may lead to hearing aids with the ability to select one voice among many
item.description: Scientists have developed an artificial hearing system that harnesses the brain's ability to amplify certain sounds while suppressing others. It could lead to better hearing aids.
item.content:encoded: Scientists have developed an artificial hearing system that harnesses the brain's ability to amplify certain sounds while suppressing others. It could lead to better hearing aids.
item.dc:creator: Jon Hamilton

item.title: Forest therapy: Why a physician wants more doctors to train in nature-based medicine
item.description: A Boston hospital fights staff burnout and boosts patient morale with forest bathing — getting people outside for some time in nature.
item.content:encoded: A Boston hospital fights staff burnout and boosts patient morale with forest bathing — getting people outside for some time in nature.
item.dc:creator: Martha Bebinger

item.title: Why cruise ship passengers with possible hantavirus exposure went to Nebraska
item.description: The University of Nebraska is home to the only federally funded quarantine unit in the U.S. and a separate biocontainment unit that can treat people exposed to infectious diseases.
item.content:encoded: The University of Nebraska is home to the only federally funded quarantine unit in the U.S. and a separate biocontainment unit that can treat people exposed to infectious diseases.
item.dc:creator: Scott Neuman

item.title: 18 passengers return to U.S. after weeks on cruise ship hit with hantavirus outbreak
item.description: Eighteen U.S. passengers have returned stateside after weeks on the cruise ship at the center of a hantavirus outbreak. They're being monitored at specialized medical facilities.
item.content:encoded: Eighteen U.S. passengers have returned stateside after weeks on the cruise ship at the center of a hantavirus outbreak. They're being monitored at specialized medical facilities.
item.dc:creator: Pien Huang

item.title: Abortion pill by mail allowed for at least 3 more days, the Supreme Court says
item.description: Full access to the abortion pill mifepristone, including through telemedicine and the mail, will continue for at least three more days, the high court said on Monday.
item.content:encoded:
Full access to the abortion pill mifepristone, including through telemedicine and the mail, will continue for at least three more days, the high court said on Monday.

(Image credit: Anna Moneymaker)
item.dc:creator: Selena Simmons-Duffin

item.title: As ranks of uninsured grow, charity care can be hard to come by at many hospitals
item.description: An investigation of hospital data and charity care programs shows most Minnesota hospitals provide little financial aid to patients and often make assistance difficult to get.
item.content:encoded:
An investigation of hospital data and charity care programs shows most Minnesota hospitals provide little financial aid to patients and often make assistance difficult to get.

(Image credit: Anthony Souffle)
item.dc:creator: Noam Levey

item.title: Marketers say NAD+ pills and infusions can boost longevity. What's the evidence?
item.description: Wellness and longevity influencers are pushing a compound called NAD+. There's scientific interest in its potential, but researchers say the marketing claims have gotten ahead of the science.
item.content:encoded: Wellness and longevity influencers are pushing a compound called NAD+. There's scientific interest in its potential, but researchers say the marketing claims have gotten ahead of the science.
item.dc:creator: Will Stone

item.title: U.S. cruise passengers arrive in the U.S. after one tests positive for hantavirus
item.description: One of the 18 Americans tested "mildly" positive for the virus during the U.S. return flight. A French woman has also tested positive — she was was among five French passengers repatriated to Paris.
item.content:encoded:
One of the 18 Americans tested "mildly" positive for the virus during the U.S. return flight. A French woman has also tested positive — she was was among five French passengers repatriated to Paris.

(Image credit: Chris McGrath)
item.dc:creator: Pien Huang

item.title: Doomscrolling too much? Try these tips to put the phone down and plug into real life
item.description: NPR Life Kit's Marielle Segarra provides tips to help you reduce your screentime.
item.content:encoded: NPR Life Kit's Marielle Segarra provides tips to help you reduce your screentime.
item.dc:creator: Marielle Segarra

item.title: A chocolate laboratory in Italy will be good for chocolate eaters — and farmers
item.description: The chocolate biz is raising the bar with a lab to assess cacao beans from around the world. (Talk about a sweet gig!) Consumers and farmers stand to benefit from the "Cacao of Excellence" program.
item.content:encoded:
The chocolate biz is raising the bar with a lab to assess cacao beans from around the world. (Talk about a sweet gig!) Consumers and farmers stand to benefit from the "Cacao of Excellence" program.

(Image credit: Valerio Muscella for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Ari Daniel
